meaning: n.拥护独裁政治者adj.俄国帝制的
examples: Russia's atrocities in Chechnya go back to the 19th century, when the diminutive but oil-rich region was annexed to the Czarist Empire after a bloody campaign of colonization.
In 1918 the Romanian majority in this former czarist province voted for union with Romania.
Compared to Prussian junkers or the landed nobility in czarist Russia the Chinese landlord was a very backward man.
Deferring indefinitely settlement of defaulted Czarist and other Soviet debt obligations to the United States (including Lend-Lease).
Young Georgii and his mother were accorded czarist honors during their trip to Russia in May 1993.
